DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

SILENT STATIONS
8 / 264
Stations that produced no GNSS observations during the window. Each row shows the most likely cause based on connection events, power events, deployment status, and observation history.
STATIONSTATUS — what we observed
IADU0xUSAPower outage — Battery exhausted during the window. Connection dropped 1 time(s) and recovered 1 time(s). Technician likely on site (cabinet door events).
LAB00xUSAPower blip — Briefly switched to backup battery 1 time(s) and recovered.
NCNB0xUSALong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.
NVTN0xUSALong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.
OHLG0xUSALong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.
ORPD0xUSALong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.
TXLR0xUSAPower blip — Briefly switched to backup battery 3 time(s) and recovered.
VABF0xUSAOffline — Went offline 1 time(s); no recovery signal received.

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

ADVISORY · 1 site(s) had an unpaired backup_battery alert
These stations were observably online throughout the window but had a sar_alert_power_source_backup_battery_total increment with no corresponding grid_power_total recovery. Likely a spurious NetCloud alert or a brief recovery whose alert was dropped. Not counted in the KPIs above.
CAWS0xUSA

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.242
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.10
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.5
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.—
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.—
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.7

T-00003768—2024-09-1912:002024-11-0612:00——2024-11-0710:03
Station: CORI1 ticket
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-709Completed
Based on device logs &gt; modem is intermittently failing to authenticate or establish a data session with the…

Based on device logs > modem is intermittently failing to authenticate or establish a data session with the Verizon network. The instability is forcing the router to take recovery actions. The attempted failover to SIM 2 failed because it contained a mismatched (AT&T) SIM card.
17:46:38 and beyond show the same carrid changed from Verizon to Not registered

Sequence of events:
17:25:10 Modem resets
17:25:40 Failover to SIM2 (and failure)
17:25:40: cp_stack_mgr: int1: Switching active SIM to slot SIM2
17:26:57 failover attempt fails
cp_stack_mgr: int1: SIM returned the following status: WRONGSIM.
WARN WAN:9ce39c94: Module FW(Verizon) / SIM Carrier(AT&T) - mismatch. ATT SIM in second SIM but two are incompatible and couldn't' connect?
4. 17:27:39 switch back successful connection to SIM 1 successful
5. 17:46:38 However "carrid changed from Verizon to Not registered" indicating underlying problem causing flap

AL 12/30/2024 - data was being sent, but no observations on grafana. Remotely connected to the receiver, noted…

AL 12/30/2024 - data was being sent, but no observations on grafana.
Remotely connected to the receiver, noted it was named "TBCC"
And no satellites were being tracked,
Rebooted via the x410, no change.
re-onboarded and changed the name to WAMW, set cartesian figures from site tracker.

Now there are no data or obs being sent.
The receiver is still accessible on http://172.17.8.83/
And it shows up on the cradlepoints client list.

Stations that produced no GNSS observations during the window. Each row shows the most likely cause based on connection events, power events, deployment status, and observation history.
STATIONSTATUS — what we observed
FIJM0xFINLong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.
RONA0xROULong-term silent — No data received in over 30 days. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.221
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.10
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.11
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.1
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.2
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.4

During an intervention on 02/24/25 The UPS beeps 3 times, every 30 seconds. The UPS was like this before and a…

During an intervention on 02/24/25
The UPS beeps 3 times, every 30 seconds.
The UPS was like this before and after the technicien replaced the battery.
Appears to indicate an internal fault. Tech was unable to clear the alarms.
The UPS has been left with the old battery, and the new one will be returned to Nokia as unused.

T-00003663GC-11 (00603-11)2024-09-1809:002024-09-1809:002024-09-1805:52—2024-10-0705:50
GCIM-314Completed
AL 5/3/2024 - Station started running on battery backup at 07:30 UTC 05/03/24 Station went offline at 09:45 UT…

AL 5/3/2024 - Station started running on battery backup at 07:30 UTC 05/03/24
Station went offline at 09:45 UTC 05/03/24

This is the 3rd outage in the past 30 days,
Initial issue:
"I send response form field engineer.

I wanted to inform you that we have identified the problem in the electrical panel of the rectifier's boiler, which was affecting the service. To resolve it, we proceeded to restart the electrical panel, which successfully restored the service. I appreciate the confirmation you have sent."

Potentially we should look at what changes could be made.
The electrical panel being modified so that our system is fed from a separate circuit?
Note that a UPS is used, so a non ground fault interrupter may be more suitable.

T-00004124GC-08 (00603-08)2025-06-1512:092025-06-1607:302025-06-1601:01—2025-08-2005:42
GCIM-518Completed
We want Nokia/Circet to return and secure the breaker for the power. Orange are also required to check the fix…

We want Nokia/Circet to return and secure the breaker for the power.

Orange are also required to check the fixed line.

Circet previously noted that the breaker is located on the ground floor, near the elevator and stairs, making it easily accessible to a wide range of individuals. They also suggested the potential installation of a lockable box to prevent intentional disruption of the breaker. Could this be further investigated and installed?

We have previously experienced interference with both power and communication links. To prevent any disruption to the station\'s operation, we would like to implement measures to protect the power supply wherever possible.

Original ticket

The station is currently offline, Suspected grid power issue.
01/22/2025 08:01:41 AM - The device entered the "offline" state.
01/22/2025 07:39:25 AM - Battery State is Exhausted!
01/22/2025 05:57:06 AM - Power Source is Backup Battery

The objective is a Nokia/Indigo GBTA site intervention.

 

The content of the job incl. needed spare parts and tools is described in the enclosed PDF handout.

The content has been pre-agreed with Indigo through Claire Johnson from Nokia.

 

The date of site visit needs to agreed with Peter Budek +49 171 2036939 (peter.budek@telekom.de)

He will provide remote back office support. Martin Rickson +44 (7542) 664498 is an alternative local contact.

T-00004236GC-13 (00603-13)2025-08-2019:022025-08-2200:002025-08-2208:48—2025-09-1506:00
GCIM-513Completed
Station is currently offline, This ticket supersedes https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-485 There is…

Station is currently offline, This ticket supersedes https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-485

There is a suspected issue with the Adel UPS within the Infrabox following a visit on 01/09/25

The adel is detective? voltage in, but not out? Screen is not on.

Therefore the GC cabinet is off, therefore the x410 has not powered the CPE/Lancom on.

However we do have power into the infrabox as the service plug has power. 

As discussed with Peter Budek, Infrabox replacement is probably the most promising option.

The station's UPS battery was replaced on 03/27/25. Approximately four hours after the technician departed the…

The station's UPS battery was replaced on 03/27/25. Approximately four hours after the technician departed the site, the station lost grid power and operated on battery backup for nearly 10 hours.

• The station is currently offline, Suspected grid power issue.
• Station appeared to start running on battery backup at approximately 15:05 UTC 03/27/25
• Battery became exhausted and the station went offline at approximately 12:43 UTC 03/28/25.

  • Station has experienced multiple short outages in the last 24hrs totaling 1.2hours, mainly on ITCO00
  • LMC – shows a high frequency of DSL (4G modem) & LTE disconnections.

  • The Adel has been stable.

The lancom intermittently connected using LTE backup, in the past week, the lancom have been on LTE backup 7 times, with a combined time of around 20 hours.

On 05/18/23 there was an intervention, the VDF modem was moved outside of the infrabox.

The station is currently offline, likely due to a grid power issue.

Please begin by performing a remote check for any power-related faults.

T-00001843GC-11 (00603-11)——2023-06-2621:10—2023-07-2113:33
GCIM-62Completed
This station has gone off-line multiple times. The original ticket, https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCI…

This station has gone off-line multiple times. The original ticket, https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-38 was closed per request by Network Ops. For that incident, the station went down 4/24/2023 and then came back up 4/25/2023 without intervention. The NOC was going to keep that ticket open when the station went down again a second time, but a new ticket was opened in SiteTracker that spawned this new ticket in JIRA. This ticket was originally closed as a duplicate but was reopened to maintain synch with ST.

The intervention by Networks! was triggered from https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-38 and also discussed during the weekly 4P Open Tickets call.

The NOC will make sure to update this ticket with the intervention information from 6/5/2023

T-00001702GC-11 (00603-11)————2023-07-0712:48

Power loss on multiple days. Has been stable since 4/25/2023

T-00003809GC-11 (00603-11)2024-12-0319:032024-12-0408:002024-12-0409:57—2024-12-0406:22
GCIM-361Completed
SVKE experienced an outage at around 04:17 – 08:49 CEST 07/28/24 The station did not appear to go on battery b…

SVKE experienced an outage at around 04:17 – 08:49 CEST 07/28/24

The station did not appear to go on battery backup prior to going offline,
The Lancom shows an outage between 04:19 - 08:49

Once it came back online, the CHC was not sending observations, I reset the Cartesian “here” and observations started being sent.

No significant NC alerts as to why the station went offline.
07/28/2024 08:48:19 AM - The device entered the "online" state.
07/28/2024 04:26:30 AM - The device entered the "offline" state.
07/28/2024 04:17:23 AM - Reboot The device has been rebooted.

Looking at the Adel Grafana page, over the past 90 days, there is evidence of the Adel going from charging to not charging, but no sign of it running on battery backup.

Excerpts from the Adel event log (please note that the times are 40 minutes behind, these lines are highlighted red in the spreadsheet, I have since synced the clock to CEST)

28/07/2024 - 08:10:10 - Display Display turned ON
28/07/2024 - 03:39:15 - Device 1 Alarm ON Low battery state of charge
28/07/2024 - 03:39:14 - Device 1 Device connected
28/07/2024 - 03:39:07 - Display Display turned ON

The config for the Adel, appears to match other stations, so I do not think there is an issue with that.
All the pictures from the install look ok, from what I can see everything looks connected etc.
I suspect that there is an issue with the Adel running on battery backup.

SWSK01 - Septentrio PolaRx5 Stopped sending Data/obs at around16:30 UTC 06/02/24 Unable to remotely reach the…

SWSK01 - Septentrio PolaRx5
Stopped sending Data/obs at around16:30 UTC 06/02/24
Unable to remotely reach the septentrio receiver, it does not appear on the cradlepoints client list. Tried rebooting receiver & cradlepoint to no avail.
Onsite intervention required, with spare GC cabinet.

TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-141Completed
Stations effected: FIEK, FIII, FIIV, FIJM, FIJO, FIKL, FIKM, FILA, FILV, FIMU, FIUU Stations initially experie…

Stations effected: FIEK, FIII, FIIV, FIJM, FIJO, FIKL, FIKM, FILA, FILV, FIMU, FIUU

Stations initially experienced outages at 18:30, intermittently lasting upto 40 minutes, some stations were then effected at 02:16 lasting upto 15 minutes.

Lancom - From a sample, some stations, a spike up up in cloud round trip time, dip in WAN sent throughput/Ethernet throughput 

SRO - From a sample, some stations showed the DSL outage, and some didn't.

Times are in CET

0
✓ every SAR-registered station produced GNSS observations in this window

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.64
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.2
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.1
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.—
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.—
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.—

Station: TQ3C1 ticket
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-292Completed
TQC3 00 CAN context: 3rd party station in Quebec located at Three Rivers Station. Every once in a while we get…

TQC3 00 CAN context: 3rd party station in Quebec located at Three Rivers Station. Every once in a while we get some really bad data from these stations here. It is unkonwn why we get bad data from it. We need to understand why. We want to narrow down if it’s the Trimble causing this issue. We may need to issue a ticket to Telus. But fist let’s run a quality analzyer on this location (station quality tool) to see if issue is specific to one system or the other. If the interference only happens wiht the trimble Rx - we issue a ticket

STATIONSTATUS — what we observed
JPSU0xJPNPower outage — Battery exhausted during the window. Connection dropped 3 time(s) and recovered 3 time(s). Technician likely on site (cabinet door events).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

STATIONSTATUSUPTIME (bar · duration · trend)DOWNTIMEPRIMARY (WAN)SECONDARY (cellular · est)CURRENT
✓ All stations stable across the window — no path-connection events.
DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.57
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.—
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.—
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.—
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.—
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.—

Station’s trimble went offline - 2026-03-14 04:07:30 UTC Receiver accessible and station healthy, suspect ante…

Station’s trimble went offline - 2026-03-14 04:07:30 UTC

2026-04-24 UPDATE: old router having connection issues when testing, opened CP case to investigate if cradlepointe is healthy (01302591)

2026-04-07 UPDATE:

New station installed under https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GC2SKY-1589

OLD THREADS

Antenna/Receiver not tracking satellites or Some Devices in Goldcrest are down

See original thread for detail:

https://snav.slack.com/archives/C05V7JM54US/p1744090578255189

3/27/26 update: new system powered on assumed installed. Working with network and tools to bring online

2026-03-19 update:

JPKO set to be replaced {{2026-03-26}}with spare router/station IBR900-5FE (already registered, tested, and ready for deployment)

11/18 update:

New priority is Okinawa for Husqvarna, dealer trying to install mower at golf course realized skylark is down there

Joseph Noronha

Just received this from KDDI. There is some hesitation regarding installation.2.Regarding the construction of the reference station (Goldcrest) in the Okinawa area
 KDDI needs to obtain approval from senior management on the business side for additional investments to commence the construction of the reference station (Goldcrest) in the Okinawa region. However, given the current sales situation (including the actual R/S results and plans from Swift), it is difficult to submit a proposal to secure this approval at this time. Additionally, KDDI’s technical team anticipates that constructing the reference station (Goldcrest) in the Okinawa area will require approximately 1.5 years. Considering the above, KDDI would like to first understand the timing at which the lack of Skylark Cx coverage in Okinawa would become a problem for Swift—that is, the point at which customer impact may occur. From this, the deadline for initiating the project will be calculated backward by approximately 1.5 years. The period from today until that deadline should be regarded as the timeframe for gathering the necessary performance data and information to present to senior management. Therefore, please inform us of the timing at which the absence of Skylark Cx coverage in Okinawa would pose a problem for Swift—that is, when customer impact may occur.⁷

Hayama Hisanaga  Oct 24th at 3:03 AM
JPKW construction is scheduled as follows. *Dates are in JST.
Nov 5: GNSS station removal ※JPKW suspended
Nov 7–Nov 30: 3G cubicle removal work
Dec 2–3: GNSS station installation work ※JPKW resumes operation@Justin Sarnoski   cc : @Daiki Kawamatsu
During the construction period mentioned above, please exclude JPKW from the monthly report calculations.Additionally, KDDI will perform post-construction functionality verification. Could you please lift the VPN restrictions for the router, P2 Elite, PolaRx5, and X410 to allow internet access, and provide the credential information?

T-00004153—2025-06-3012:00———2025-11-1906:24
Station: JPRS3 tickets
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-1064Open
JPRS/IBR900-42e Station has had power issues starting in April 2026, and has been offline for 20% of the month…

JPRS/IBR900-42e Station has had power issues starting in April 2026, and has been offline for 20% of the month. This pattern is continuing into May, with it offline over 50% of time

Station was replaced in December 2025, and it also had antenna repair January 2026 ( GCIM-964 Completed ), but these do not appear to be related; since these new power issues appear to have started in April.

Enhanced logging has been enabled on the device, but no errors allude to any hardware issues. The device simply powers off.

Temperature was noted as low for station, but no errors in logs to denote issues (logs show modem temp as nominal), and other devices are in much colder climates and operating within spec.

T-00004647GC-11 (00603-11)2026-04-0419:002026-04-0419:002026-04-1000:30——
GCIM-1039Completed
Started 2026-02-06 SIM1 error - no sim found resulting in NO CARRIER and failover to SIM2: 1970-01-01T01:01:50…

Started 2026-02-06

SIM1 error - no sim found resulting in NO CARRIER and failover to SIM2:
1970-01-01T01:01:50+00:00,INFO,WAN:1221ab15b,Execute Event ok: 10
1970-01-01T01:02:12+00:00,INFO,cp_stack_mgr,int1: Error no SIM. Verify SIM card is inserted properly.
1970-01-01T01:02:12+00:00,INFO,cp_stack_mgr,int1: SIM returned the following status: NOSIM.
1970-01-01T01:02:12+00:00,INFO,WAN:1221ab15b,Preconfigured Event: user intervention required
1970-01-01T01:02:12+00:00,INFO,WAN:1221ab15b,Configure Event: user intervention require

After reboot, it will try SIm1 then get the errors above and throw a failover alert and move to SIM2.

RESOLVING ISSUE AS RECOVERED

T-00004483GC-10 (00603-10)2025-11-2909:442025-12-0300:002025-12-0307:11—2026-01-0606:07
Station: JPSD1 ticket
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-171Completed
SIM2 appears to not have taken up connection (CP contends that these are Carrier DNS problems), manually faile…

SIM2 appears to not have taken up connection (CP contends that these are Carrier DNS problems), manually failed to SIM2 to test card and it works now)

Not a power event, logs show offline

CP tickets:

Original Cradlepoint Assessment:
Regarding case 01289485; I have attached more cleaned up logs to better identify what happened. In the last case DNS was called as as root cause, but none of our devices use DynamicDNS and should not be dependent on that service.

Looking at the logs it appears to be an odd Timesync problem or system hang where the logs 'lose' 5 hours (see note in log), it then starts using SIM2, throws USB errors and can't connect for several hours before finally trying SIM1 again and connecting.

My question is, is this problem with SIM2 a carrier issue or a local hardware problem we should send someone out to reseat or otherwise check? This unit does not appear to ever have used SIM2, so wondering if we should fault the carrier or the hardware (card itself or the connection to the card)

Re-escalation/Confirmation (01289485+01293658)
SWIFT: I have attached more cleaned up logs to better identify what happened. In the last case DNS was called as as root cause, but none of our devices use DynamicDNS and should not be dependent on that service.
Looking at the logs it appears to be an odd Timesync problem or system hang where the logs 'lose' 5 hours (see note in log), it then starts using SIM2, throws USB errors and can't connect for several hours before finally trying SIM1 again and connecting.
My question is, is this problem with SIM2 a carrier issue or a local hardware problem we should send someone out to reseat or otherwise check? This unit does not appear to ever have used SIM2, so wondering if we should fault the carrier or the hardware (card itself or the connection to the card)

CP: Hey Justin, would you by chance be able to increase logging levels / log to usb or we could enable persistent logging? We would want to see what happened while the device was offline.
Any reboots clear the running logs, so that would cause our gaps.

SWIFT: Also is there a way to manually FORCE the cradlepoint to use SIM2 so we can check the sim and rule it out?

GCIM-824Open
4/27 update: swapped out in February but looks like so GC2Sky move over, created https://swift-nav.atlassian.n…

4/27 update: swapped out in February but looks like so GC2Sky move over, created https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GC2SKY-1595 to change config and get this back into traffic

SIM carrier events due potentially to bad cellular antenna. Both SIMS frequently see signal loss:

examples:

2025-10-22T16:39:55+00:00,INFO,WAN:98d8ae48,"signal Internal 600M (SIM2) on port int1: SS: 0%, RSSI:-125(dBm), Service: Unknown"
2025-10-22T16:41:39+00:00,INFO,WAN:98aae791,"signal Internal 600M (SIM1) on port int1: SS: 0%, RSSI:-125(dBm), Service: Unknown"

Cradlepoint advises fix antenna and replace SIM1 just to be safe.

00004102GC-10 (00603-10)——2025-12-1104:38——
GCIM-680Cancelled
Resolving in lieu of new https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-824 Request for maintenance GC ticket UPD…

Resolving in lieu of new https://swift-nav.atlassian.net/browse/GCIM-824 Request for maintenance GC ticket

UPDATE AS OF SEPT 19. 2025

Pushed back to KDDI for advice on next step: @Daiki Kawamatsu for JPSU outages/failovers

https://snav.slack.com/archives/C04B5T35HB7/p1758181149688749?thread_ts=1748973287.268929&cid=C04B5T35HB7

Station is still experiencing failovers even after a firmware update
Station is not dual backhaul so more at risk of failover outages
Station outages are directly related to SIM failovers:

Logs sent to Cradlepoint (CP ticket 01191048) resulted in their service tech identifying root cause as SIM1 CPPM carrier issues:

Cradlepoint advises a SIM1 replacement (or a full modem replacement) to correct:

At this point we have exhausted any remote work that can be done to triage this station, and any further troubleshooting to avoid failover will require an onsite presence to:
(a) replace SIM1 to rule out the CPPM/simcard as the root cause (and possibly replace modem too)
(b) replace SIM1 + add an additional modem to bring the unit up to a dual backhaul standard to avoid future downtime issues from backhaul problems
(c) accept the current impact as within SLA (~4 outages a month, all under 20 minute duration)

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

✓ All stations stable across the window — no path-connection events.
DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.20
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.—
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.—
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.—
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.—
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.—

the failure is an obvious power failure (see attached screen capture). What is interesting is that I do not see the typical (on battery power message at the start of the power failure). we can diagnose this once the station is back online.

Station: KTHM2 tickets
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-730Completed
7/16/2025 The device logs indicate the power source's backup battery was activated around 10:44 PM UTC and was…

7/16/2025

The device logs indicate the power source's backup battery was activated around 10:44 PM UTC and was fully depleted by 7:40 PM UTC.

Sequence of event:
07/16/2025 10:44:22 PM UTC
-GPIO State Change
-Power Source is Backup Battery, pin: 3, value: 1
07/16/2025 10:45:24 PM UTC
-Failover Event
-Failover event detected at 2025-07-16T22:44:16.377022Z. WAN interface changed fr
07/16/2025 11:40:02 PM UTC
-GPIO State Change
-Battery State is Exhausted!, pin: 4, value: 1

-Station : KTHM01KOR

KTHM is currently offline, it appeared to run on battery backup before going offline, therefore suggesting the…

KTHM is currently offline, it appeared to run on battery backup before going offline, therefore suggesting there is an issue with the grid power.

Station: KTHS1 ticket
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-18Completed
The station at KTHS was offline twice on 2/7/2023. Looks like a power issue, but the UPS didn't keep the stati…

The station at KTHS was offline twice on 2/7/2023.  Looks like a power issue, but the UPS didn't keep the station on, nor did it report off grid power.  Please perform a check that everything is connected correctly, and the UPS is functioning.

Station: KTYY1 ticket
TicketStatusDescriptionST #SKUStartDetectEscalatedEndClosed
GCIM-9Completed
While attempting to onboard KTYY to DMVPN, it was determined that the x410’s configuration account is inaccess…

While attempting to onboard KTYY to DMVPN, it was determined that the x410’s configuration account is inaccessible. After preforming all remote troubleshooting procedures it was determined that the x410 requires a Factory Reset. This procedure can only be done onsite by physically reseting the x410.

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

DURATION OF EMERGENCY POWER · per station · time on backup battery during window

Each event = one transition from grid power → backup battery (a single grid-loss). Multiple events at the same site = multiple separate outages (or repeated grid flickers). Duration shown is time without grid power, not necessarily time on battery — outages longer than the UPS battery life mean the station was offline for most of that time. Sources: ac_input_voltage (Adel UPS · direct measurement) and sar_alert_power_source_* (Back-UPS Pro · event-pair reconstruction).

PATH CONNECTIONS · station connection health

Each row = one station. Uptime = how long it was reachable. Downtime = how long it was fully offline. Sources: sar_status_connection_online gauge for uptime/downtime · sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters for failover events. Note: per-SIM detail (SIM1 vs SIM2) is not yet available — Prometheus does not distinguish slot. Cellular session durations (used internally for classification) are estimated by event timing.

DATA ACCURACY
ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.
STATUS GUIDE
StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.17
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.1
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.—
FLAPPINGSwitched to cellular many times and recovered each time (5+). Wired is unstable.—
STUCK / degradedSwitched to cellular but didn't come back cleanly. Station is degraded.—
ON CELLULARProbably running on cellular backup right now (best guess).—
OFFLINE (entire window)Station was unreachable the entire window. Likely decommissioned or hardware failure.—

Etisalat has 10 telemetered stations in this window, but 10 are flagged non-production in sitetracker (noc_status != "1") — so the production roster is empty and headline KPIs cannot be computed. ColumnSourceConfidenceNotes
Uptime / Downtimesar_status_connection_online gauge · 60s step✓ DIRECTMeasured. Resolution ±60s per state transition.
Failover events (mdm / online / offline)sar_alert_connection_state_*_total counters✓ DIRECTCounter increments emitted by NetCloud on state-change alerts.
Primary (WAN) duration= uptime − cellular duration↔ INFERREDDerived from #1 and #4. Accuracy bounded by #4.
Secondary (Cellular) durationper-mdm event session timing, capped at 4h≈ ESTIMATEDBest-effort: each mdm event timed to the next online/offline event. Sessions longer than 4h are capped (NetCloud rarely reports continuous sessions).
Current connection (WAN / Cellular / Offline)instant gauge + last-2h event window✓ DIRECTWAN/Offline are gauge-direct. Cellular detection is inferred: online now + mdm event in last 2h + more mdm than online events.
Trend arrow (▲▼·)comparison vs same weekday last week (T-7)✓ DIRECTSame gauge over same hour-of-week window.
Per-SIM split (SIM1 vs SIM2)not exposed by Prometheus✗ UNAVAILABLERequires a swift-nav/sar PR to surface per-slot metrics. Not in this report.
CONFIDENCE LABELS
✓ DIRECTObserved. Read straight from a Prometheus gauge or counter at query time. Highest trust.
↔ INFERREDDeduced. Derived via fixed logic from other DIRECT measurements (e.g. primary = uptime − cellular). Logically correct if its inputs are — trace the source chain to gauge trust.
≈ ESTIMATEDApproximated. Numeric best-effort when no direct metric exists (e.g. cellular session = mdm event → next event, capped at 4 h). Carries an unknown error margin.
✗ UNAVAILABLENot exposed by any data source we can read. Requires upstream work (SAR PR, NetCloud API) before we can surface it.

StatusWhat it meansCount
stableNo problems. Station stayed on its main connection the whole time.4
outage, no failoverWent offline but cellular backup didn't kick in. Worth checking.—
brief failoverSwitched to cellular a few times (1–4). Wired connection had small blips.—
